timeline.label: FY2025 year-end: board proposes no dividend; reinvestment-first capital policy reaffirmed
Nine bolt-on acquisitions closed in FY2025; portfolio expands to 38 subsidiaries
Goodwill impairment 73 MSEK in Q3 flags past overpayment on legacy deals
Tail turnaround: deliberate exits drive organic net sales −5% while EBITA margin rises to 11.3%
UK expansion accelerates with seven acquisitions; Teqnion UK Ltd established
Spiltan remains largest holder at 11.41%; Antheia (Daniel Ek) block reported via Vixar sale
Decentralized serial-acquirer model under CEO Steene; CXO role for acquisition general
Nasdaq First North Growth Market listing via IPO
Teqnion founded as industrial serial acquirer platform

industry.structure: Fragmented European niche-industrial suppliers; Teqnion aggregates local leaders through decentralized ownership with parent-level capital allocation.
industry.share_shift: Portfolio grows via bolt-on M&A (nine deals FY2025) while pruning underperformers; organic sales −5% reflects deliberate exits not market share loss at core units.
industry.trend: Margin inflection (EBITA 11.3% FY2025, Q1 2026 14.3% per prior interim) and FCF recovery; UK build-out and earn-out liabilities increase balance-sheet complexity.
